ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301710 covers orange juice drink as a category; no exact entry for Minute Maid Refresh Orange branded product exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers ready-to-drink orange juice; Minute Maid Refresh Orange is a flavoured orange juice drink with added sugar, so closest_parent is appropriate.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229961001 is the nearest available concept; the branded Minute Maid Refresh Orange product is not individually coded in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Minute Maid Refresh Orange is approximately 350 ml (350 g), equivalent to one retail bottle or a single-serve cup as dispensed at a restaurant such as Sukiya.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, orange drink, breakfast type, with juice, canned — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14242)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Minute Maid Refresh Orange is a commercially produced orange juice drink with added sugar and vitamin C fortification. USDA SR Legacy entry 14242 (orange drink, breakfast type, with juice, canned) is the closest approved analogue. Values are adapted from this entry. Energy ~46 kcal/100 ml, carbohydrate ~11.5 g/100 ml, sugar ~11.0 g/100 ml, protein ~0.1 g/100 ml, fat ~0.1 g/100 ml, sodium ~5 mg/100 ml, dietary fibre ~0.1 g/100 ml. These are consistent with typical Minute Maid orange drink formulations.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Minute Maid Refresh Orange specifically. GI of 66 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for orange juice (reconstituted from concentrate), which is the closest approved analogue (GI ~65–66, medium category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 40.25) / 100 ≈ 26.6, rounded to 26.8 using 11.5 g/100 g × 350 g serving = 40.25 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~5 mg/100 ml); potassium present at ~35 mg/100 ml
hypertension.guidance: Minute Maid Refresh Orange is low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertension risk from sodium load; however, its high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk factors that indirectly elevate blood pressure with regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~11 g/100 ml) and medium glycemic index (~66) producing a high glycemic load per serving (~26.8)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Minute Maid Refresh Orange due to its high free sugar content and medium-to-high glycemic load per serving, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; a 350 ml serving delivers approximately 40 g of rapidly absorbed carbohydrate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk; negligible saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened orange juice drinks like Minute Maid Refresh Orange may raise triglyceride levels and contribute to cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake and prefer whole fruit or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Minute Maid Refresh Orange is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink orange-flavoured juice drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company under the Minute Maid brand. It is made from water, orange juice concentrate, added sugar, citric acid, natural orange flavouring, and ascorbic acid (vitamin C fortification). The product is ultra-processed and is distinct from pure squeezed orange juice in that it contains added sugars and flavour enhancers. It has a sweet, tangy orange taste with a light, watery texture typical of diluted juice drinks. In Singapore, it is served as a beverage option at Japanese fast-food restaurants such as Sukiya. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 46 kcal, 11.5 g carbohydrate (predominantly free sugars ~11 g), 0.1 g protein, and 0.1 g fat. Sodium is low at ~5 mg/100 ml. It is fortified with vitamin C (~38 mg/100 ml). The glycemic index is approximately 66 (medium), and a standard 350 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 26.8, making it a high-glycemic-load beverage per serving. It is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or those on sugar-restricted diets. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: No exact branded nutritional data for Minute Maid Refresh Orange sold in Singapore was available from an approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 14242 (orange drink, breakfast type, with juice, canned), which is the closest approved analogue. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for reconstituted orange juice. Confidence is medium rather than low because the analogue is well-matched in product type and composition.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 14242 (orange drink, breakfast type, with juice, canned) as no exact branded entry for Minute Maid Refresh Orange Singapore was available in an approved primary source. 2. The input record assigned cuisine 'Japanese' because this product is served at Sukiya (a Japanese fast-food chain); however, as the source is 'field survey data' and the product is widely sold in Singapore, multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been applied in addition to Japanese, to ensure compliance with Veritas v2 requirements. The product itself originates from the United States. 3. Saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA analogue source; this is a beverage with negligible fat content. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA analogue source. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA analogue source. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. 4. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA analogue source for this product type. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 5. Protein_g (0.1) and fat_g (0.1) are not zero — trace amounts are present per the USDA analogue. 6. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on reconstituted orange juice from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Minute Maid Refresh Orange was found. 7. The cuisine_tags in the input (Beef Dish, Japanese Curry, Grilled Chicken Skewers) are restaurant-level tags for Sukiya and are not applicable to this beverage product; they have been discarded.

improvement_actions_taken: Stripped menu numbering '655.' from food_name to produce canonical English name.
Corrected food_group from 'Mixed' to 'Beverages' as this is a ready-to-drink beverage.
Added Japanese local name per G6 multilingual rule for Japanese cuisine.

Added Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to comply with Singaporean multilingual rules, as the product is sold in Singapore and the source is field survey data, despite the input cuisine being Japanese.
Updated region_of_origin to 'United States (globally distributed, sold in Japan and Singapore)' for better accuracy.
Sourced nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 14242 as closest approved analogue.
Assigned GI of 66 from University of Sydney GI Database analogue (reconstituted orange juice).
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes.
Documented zero values (saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg) as confirmed zero per source.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Discarded irrelevant cuisine_tags (Beef Dish, Japanese Curry, Grilled Chicken Skewers) from input.
Set ontology match_type values to only allowed values: exact, closest_parent, or none.
